meta.building: Flèche de la cathédrale Notre-Dame de Paris
meta.date_built: spire designed/erected 1859 (Viollet-le-Duc); lost in the fire 15 Apr 2019; rebuilt à l'identique, cathedral reopened 7–8 Dec 2024; new spire summit + rooster 2023
meta.generated_by: scripts/derive-notre-dame-whole.mjs (Yingzao Rule Engine — whole-cathedral composer; PASS 1 massing + spire)
meta.composition: ONE spec composed from per-fragment scopes; massing fragments offset/namespaced to world, the spire composed in UNCHANGED at the crossing [0,0,0].

units.note: All positions/dimensions in metres. World frame: crossing at origin, +X→chevet, −X→west front, ±Z→transept arms.

rule_derived_split.note: Crossing position along the 128 m, nave-vs-choir split, bay interval, wall depths, tower footprints and aisle/roof heights are rule_derived (corpus does not publish them) — never measured.

components.role: The west façade screen — 43.5 m wide and 45 m tall (both measured, excluding the towers). Its wall depth is rule_derived (not published). The Gallery of Kings / rose / 3 portals are a later fidelity tier.
components.note: Façade width 43.5 m + height 45 m MEASURED (FRIENDS layout; techno-science.net). Wall depth is rule_derived (GAP). X position is rule_derived (façade at the -X end of the 128 m).

components.role: West tower rising to 69 m (measured height). Its footprint and spacing along Z are rule_derived (not published). Tower pinnacles / belfry openings are a later fidelity tier.
components.note: Tower height 69 m MEASURED (de.Wikipedia + FRIENDS). Tower footprint ~11.5 m and ±Z spacing are rule_derived (GAP _GAP_wall_depths). '297 steps' was REFUTED → not used.

components.role: West tower rising to 69 m (measured height). Its footprint and spacing along Z are rule_derived (not published). Tower pinnacles / belfry openings are a later fidelity tier.
components.note: Tower height 69 m MEASURED (de.Wikipedia + FRIENDS). Tower footprint ~11.5 m and ±Z spacing are rule_derived (GAP _GAP_wall_depths). '297 steps' was REFUTED → not used.

components.role: The central nave vessel — 13 m clear width, 33 m high-vault crown (both measured). Its 49 m length and crossing offset are rule_derived (10 bays × ~4.9 m; the nave-vs-choir split is not published).
components.note: Vessel width 13 m + vault crown 33 m MEASURED (FRIENDS layout / techno-science / de.Wikipedia). Length 49 m over 10 measured bays (interval ~4.9 m) is rule_derived. MEASURED-REALITY GUARD: 33 m is the crown, never 35 m / 43 m.

components.role: Inner side aisle — 5.9 m clear width (measured). Its vault crown height is not published (corpus gap) → rendered at a rule_derived height below the 33 m vessel crown.
components.note: Aisle width 5.9 m MEASURED (FRIENDS layout). Aisle vault crown height is a GAP (_GAP_aisle_vault_heights) → height is rule_derived, never asserted as measured.

components.role: Inner side aisle — 5.9 m clear width (measured). Its vault crown height is not published (corpus gap) → rendered at a rule_derived height below the 33 m vessel crown.
components.note: Aisle width 5.9 m MEASURED (FRIENDS layout). Aisle vault crown height is a GAP (_GAP_aisle_vault_heights) → height is rule_derived, never asserted as measured.

components.role: The square-ish transept crossing on which the flèche is anchored — the world origin. Width 14 m is measured (transept arm width); its X centre = the origin is rule_derived (crossing position along the 128 m is not published).
components.note: Width 14 m MEASURED (transept arm width, FRIENDS+de.Wikipedia). The crossing CENTRE at the origin is rule_derived — the corpus does not publish the crossing position along the 128 m total length.

components.role: Transept arm reaching ±24 m from the crossing (48 m arm-to-arm, measured); 14 m wide along X (measured). Length to ±24 m is measured-derived from the 48 m width.
components.note: Arm-to-arm 48 m + arm width 14 m MEASURED (FRIENDS layout). The arm spans the crossing centre out to ±24 m (measured-derived from the 48 m width).

components.role: Transept arm reaching ±24 m from the crossing (48 m arm-to-arm, measured); 14 m wide along X (measured). Length to ±24 m is measured-derived from the 48 m width.
components.note: Arm-to-arm 48 m + arm width 14 m MEASURED (FRIENDS layout). The arm spans the crossing centre out to ±24 m (measured-derived from the 48 m width).

components.role: The choir vessel east of the crossing — same 13 m vessel width and 33 m vault crown as the nave (measured). Its length and the nave-vs-choir split are rule_derived (not published).
components.note: Vessel width 13 m + vault crown 33 m MEASURED. Choir straight-bay length 37.06 m and the nave/choir split are rule_derived (the corpus publishes no clean modern split).

components.role: Choir side aisle continuing the 5.9 m aisle east toward the double ambulatory (measured width; height a rule_derived gap).
components.note: Aisle width 5.9 m MEASURED. Height a rule_derived GAP. Radiating chapels / double ambulatory detail is a later fidelity tier.

components.role: Choir side aisle continuing the 5.9 m aisle east toward the double ambulatory (measured width; height a rule_derived gap).
components.note: Aisle width 5.9 m MEASURED. Height a rule_derived GAP. Radiating chapels / double ambulatory detail is a later fidelity tier.

components.role: The semicircular chevet closing the east end — outer radius from the measured ~47.88 m chevet diameter. The hemicycle FORM is rule_derived massing (the published Bork/Tallon concentric radii trace to a rights-excluded blog → GAP, never rendered as measured).
components.note: Outer radius from the chevet outer diameter ~47.88 m (MEASURED, = the 48 m width); the hemicycle FORM is rule_derived (ad-quadratum half-revolve). The Bork/Tallon concentric chevet radii (CHARTRES-BLOG, rights_excluded) are a GAP and are NOT rendered. The radiating chapels / double ambulatory are a later fidelity tier.

components.role: Steep roof slope over the nave + choir, ridge near the 43 m under-roof level (measured). The steep two-slope ridge form is rule_derived massing; the lost oak 'forêt' frame is a later conjecture tier.
components.note: Ridge crest near the 43 m under-roof level (MEASURED, FRIENDS layout); the steep two-slope ridge FORM is rule_derived (the lost 13th-c. oak 'forêt' frame is a later conjecture tier). Rendered as two roof slopes.

components.role: Steep roof slope over the nave + choir, ridge near the 43 m under-roof level (measured). The steep two-slope ridge form is rule_derived massing; the lost oak 'forêt' frame is a later conjecture tier.
components.note: Ridge crest near the 43 m under-roof level (MEASURED, FRIENDS layout); the steep two-slope ridge FORM is rule_derived (the lost 13th-c. oak 'forêt' frame is a later conjecture tier). Rendered as two roof slopes.

components.role: The timber stool transferring the spire's load onto the four crossing piers.
components.note: single medium-confidence footprint source → conjecture (cross-check only)

components.role: Octagonal souche of the flèche, lead over an oak armature.
components.note: octagonal form + proportion from the VLD PD 'Flèche' plate; absolute height scaled off the plate × the measured 66 m extent

components.role: Horizontal ring beam tying the eight oak arêtes into the octagon at this level.
components.note: Octagon radius from the ad quadratum rule; exact springing dimension is a GAP (rule_derived, not surveyed).

Pinnacle set out by the 1486 Roriczer rule — base square, inscribed squares at 45°.
components.note: Shaft height = base × 6 (Roriczer vertical multiple) — CITED-UNCERTAIN, re-verify against the verbatim 1847 text.

components.role: Tapering cap of the pinnacle — its vertical multiple (~7×) is cited-uncertain.
components.note: Auszug height from the ~7× Roriczer multiple — cited-uncertain, so flagged conjecture (uncertainty propagates).

components.role: Winged evangelist symbol leading an apostle group up the base of the spire.
components.note: Count, heights and arrangement are measured (PERSEE-2009 + corroboration); the figurative massing is stylized, not a sculpture reconstruction. Weights cited-uncertain (see canonical).

components.role: Copper apostle climbing the base of the flèche toward heaven.
Gilt orb at the foot of the rooster, capping the needle.
The gilded copper rooster at the very summit — a reliquary holding three relics. Position from the measured 96 m total height; its published weight (~30 vs ~10 kg) is cited-uncertain.
components.note: Single topmost component, at the measured 96 m. Weight cited-uncertain (V13 → conjecture).
